concept.title: Getting through the first week in Stardew Valley
concept.shortdesc: Stardew Valley is a game in which players get to play without guidelines or clear goals, allowing them to explore the world around them on their own. This document will explain what players should do to make the most out of the first week on their farm. This game is provided by ConcernedApe.

conbody.p: The first week in Stardew Valley can be confusing for beginners, as they have to get used to the controls, the mechanics and to the life in Pelican Town. Still, this first week sets the pace for the rest of the game.Therefore, starting out well is absolutely necessary

section.title: Early priorities
section.p: During the first week, players often focus on planting the starting seeds, watering crops, and collecting simple resources such as wood, stone, and forage items.

note.p: Rainy days are a good time to explore, forage, or fish because you do not need to spend time watering crops.

section.title: Learning the routine
section.p: The early game also teaches the player how to balance daily activities, shop openings, and the passing of time. This first routine helps new players understand how to plan their days.

example.title: A first day
example.p: A player might plant and water crops in the morning, collect nearby foraged items, and then explore town before shops close for the day.

section.title: Starting tools
section.p: At the start of the game, the player gains access to a set of basic tools to help perform different tasks. Over time, you will gain access to more tools. Most players keep them in their hotbar to access them easily, but they can also be stored in chests.

ul.li: Watering can: Used to water crops to make them grow.
Hoe: Used to till soil to plant Seeds.
Axe: Used to chop down trees.
Fishing rod: Used to catch fish.
Scythe: Used to cut grass and weeds. Also used to harvest specific crops (Amaranth, Kale, Wheat).

section.p: Keep in mind that tools can be upgraded at the Blacksmith in exchange for metals bars and gold.
